Marine Le Pen exploits Stade de France controversy to awaken her electorate                The presidential election finalist and her right-hand man, Jordan Bardella, are taking advantage of last weekend's chaos at the Stade de France to revive a sluggish campaign for the legislative elections.

War in Ukraine: One hundred days that have reshaped Europe  One hundred days ago, on February 24, thousands of Russian troops were assembled on the border with Ukraine. They received their orders, they invaded. One hundred days later, Ukrainian President Zelensky says 'victory will be ours.'

President Joe Biden appeals for tougher gun laws: 'How much more carnage?'  The American president delivered on Thursday, June 2, an impassioned plea - broadcasted live on major TV channels - to Congress to act on gun control, after a string of mass shootings in the country.
Subscribers onlyIndia: Umar Khalid, a symbolic prisoner of arbitrary justice  Human rights groups denounce the abuse of anti-terrorism laws to silence dissent.

Long Read   Gaspard Dhellemmes Subscribers onlyIn France, Billon Ung struggles to uncover the truth about her husband's disappearance in Cambodia In April 1975, the Khmer Rouge were close to taking power in Cambodia. Faced with the threat, Billon Ung left her country, leaving behind her husband, the president of the Cambodian National Assembly Ung Boun Hor. She would never see him again. Convinced that France had handed him over to Pol Pot's men, she is fighting for the government to acknowledge its responsibility.   13 min read

Investigation   Margherita Nasi Subscribers only'Our bodies are shared on a huge scale': Online 'fisha' accounts are cybersexism's new frontier On social networks or messaging apps, 'fisha' accounts, which publish intimate photos and videos of young women without their consent, have exploded in recent years. NGOs are trying to help victims of this type of revenge porn.   8 min read

Feature   Lilia Blaise Tennis: In Tunisia, the exciting Ons Jabeur effect Despite her first-round exit at the French Open, the tennis player, ranked sixth in the world, is proving inspirational to many young female players in her country.   3 min read

In Depth   Claire Legros Subscribers onlyDeliberation: The democratic catalyst that must be organized and cannot be improvised Contrary to conceptions of democracy that reduce political decision-making to the counting of votes, deliberative theory relies on the capacity of citizens to find solutions together, through information and discussion.   3 min read
